summary: Post-hysterectomy, the woman developed urinary continence issues indicated likely due to a vesicovaginal fistula based on positive methylene blue staining above the urethra, suggesting an abnormal connection between the bladder and vagina that occurred during surgery. Key findings excluded other types of fistulas given their specific symptomatology not matching hers exactly post-surgery. To confirm diagnosis further imaging would be needed but initial presentation aligns closely enough clinically without it at present time point. Vesicovaginal fistula remains most probable cause here according to current information provided about case scenario details available thus far discussed among agents involved so far including
